headline: Malaysia Has No National Noise Pollution Law Despite 15 Years of Drafting
context: The proposed National Noise Pollution Act has been in drafting since 2011 and has not been tabled in Parliament, leaving Malaysia without comprehensive noise regulation.

cards.big: Malaysia has been drafting a noise pollution law for 15 years.
cards.sub: 15 years. Zero readings in Parliament. Construction noise complaints rose 230% in the same period.

cards.big: DOE's noise guidelines are advisory and unenforceable.
cards.sub: Without legislation, noise complaints are handled as nuisance under local council bylaws — maximum fine RM250.

cards.big: Construction noise affects 3.2 million urban Malaysians daily.
cards.sub: WHO links chronic noise exposure to cardiovascular disease, cognitive impairment, and sleep disorders.

cards.big: Japan enacted noise control legislation in 1968. Singapore in 1999.
cards.sub: Malaysia's delay is exceptional among comparable economies.

cards.big: 15 years of drafting with zero legislative action. This is not slow progress. It is active avoidance.
Noise pollution is a measurable, manageable environmental health hazard. The technology exists, the standards exist, the health evidence exists. Only the law does not.
